enormous: adjective. Deviating from the norm; unusual, extraordinary.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
eros: noun. A winged figure of a child representing love or its power.
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).
menu: noun. The details of the food to be served at a banquet; a bill of fare.
meous: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of meou
meson: noun. The mesial plane dividing the body into similar right and left halves.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
mons: noun. One of the fleshy areas at the base of the fingers; a mount.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
moron: noun. A stupid person; an idiot; a fool.
morose: adjective. Sullen, gloomy; showing a brooding ill humour.
morse: noun. A clasp or fastening used to fasten a cope in the front, usually decorative.
mos: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mooré.
moue: noun. A pout, especially as expressing mock-annoyance or flirtatiousness.
mourn: verb. To express sadness or sorrow for; to grieve over (especially a death).
mouse: noun. Any mammal of the many rodent families (especially Muridae) that have a small body and a long tail.
mouser: noun. A cat that catches mice, kept specifically for the purpose.
mun: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Munda languages.
muon: noun. An unstable elementary particle in the lepton family, having similar properties to the electron but with a mass 207 times greater.
mus: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Creek.
muse: noun. Of a person: a source of inspiration.
noose: noun. An adjustable loop of rope, such as the one placed around the neck in hangings, or the one at the end of a lasso.
nooser: noun. Someone who uses a noose.
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
norm: noun. That which is normal or typical.
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
nose: noun. A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
nous: noun. The mind or intellect, reason, both rational and emotional.
nurse: noun. A person involved in providing direct care for the sick: Anyone performing this role, regardless of training or profession.
nus: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nuer.
omen: noun. Something which portends or is perceived to portend either a good or evil event or circumstance in the future, or which causes a
omer: noun. A former small Hebrew unit of dry volume equal to about 2.3 L or 2.1 quarts.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
onerous: adjective. Imposing or constituting a physical, mental, or figurative load which can be borne only with effort; burdensome.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.
rose: noun. A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
rum: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Romanian.
rumen: noun. The first compartment of the stomach of a cow or other ruminants.
run: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kirundi.
rune: noun. A letter, or character, used in the written language of various ancient Germanic peoples, especially the Scandinavians and the
ruse: noun. A turning or doubling back, especially of animals to get out of the way of hunting dogs.
sen: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nanerige.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
snore: verb. To breathe during sleep with harsh, snorting noises caused by vibration of the soft palate.
some: pronoun. A certain number (multiple but not all).
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels
soon: adjective. Short in length of time from the present.
sooner: adjective. comparative form of soon: more soon
sore: adjective. Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.
sorn: verb. to impose upon another for food and lodging.
sou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Thai.
sure: adjective. Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
unmoor: verb. To unfix or unsecure (a moored boat).
unmoors: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of unmoor
urn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Uruangnirin.
user: noun. One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer or client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
